title: Update CI tests to run python docker library against API
body:
* Update reference to docker-py to docker to reflect change in library
  name
* Update tests to create storage sandbox
* Enable all tests that endpoints support
* Refactor containers/{id}/rename to return 404 not 500
* Refactor tests to use quay.io vs. docker.io

Signed-off-by: Jhon Honce <jhonce@redhat.com>

comments.body:

@baude To reproduce issue:

```shell
DEBUG=1 PODMAN=./bin/podman python3 -m unittest -v \
test.python.docker.test_containers.TestContainers.test_inspect_container 2>&1 |tee /tmp/errors
```
@cevich It looks like we need a `dnf install python3-docker` on the images

> It looks like we need a dnf install python3-docker on the images

This is not normally a problem...just that right now I've got 6 browser tabs open, tracking major image-updates related for Ubuntu and and Fedora across three repositories :scream: 

Hmmm, for the short-term I think it's okay to `dnf install` it right before CI runs your tests, along with a comment to remove it once `python3-docker` is in the VM images.  That'll reduce the short-term testing burden on me, get you the package, though at the cost of some medium/long-term instability.  However, I intend to take care of that instability w/in the coming weeks (I'll add a jira card).
I can't ack the python bits, but the rest LGTM
